expectations.text: Uses a View component as the root container with explicit width and height
expectations.evidence: Line 16: `<View style={{ width: 1920, height: 1080 }}>` — root container with explicit width and height.

expectations.text: Uses InputStream component to display the video stream
expectations.evidence: Line 19: `<InputStream inputId="live_stream" />` — InputStream is used inside a Rescaler to display the video.

expectations.text: Uses Text component from @swmansion/smelter for the lower-third text
expectations.evidence: Line 2 imports Text: `import { View, Text, InputStream, Rescaler } from "@swmansion/smelter";`. Lines 35-53 use two `<Text>` components for the overlay content.

expectations.text: The text overlay is positioned at the bottom of the screen using absolute positioning (top/left/bottom/right props on a child View)
expectations.evidence: Lines 23-33: child View uses `position: "absolute", bottom: 0, left: 0` to position the overlay at the bottom of the screen.

expectations.text: Includes a backgroundColor with alpha channel for semi-transparency (e.g. #00000080 or similar)
expectations.evidence: Line 30: `backgroundColor: "#000000AA"` — hex color with AA alpha channel for semi-transparency.

expectations.text: Text component has fontSize set (fontSize is required in TextStyleProps)
expectations.evidence: Line 37: `fontSize: 36` on the first Text, and line 47: `fontSize: 24` on the second Text. Both Text components have fontSize set.

expectations.text: The code shows the correct import pattern from @swmansion/smelter
expectations.evidence: Line 1: `import Smelter from "@swmansion/smelter-node";` and Line 2: `import { View, Text, InputStream, Rescaler } from "@swmansion/smelter";` — correct pattern with Smelter engine from smelter-node and components from smelter.

claims.claim: Smelter uses a React-like JSX component model with View, Text, InputStream, Rescaler
claims.evidence: The code demonstrates this pattern and the transcript confirms it. The import from @swmansion/smelter is correct.

claims.claim: Answered purely from built-in knowledge with no documentation lookups
claims.evidence: Metrics show 0 read, 0 grep, 0 glob, 0 skill calls. Only bash (1 mkdir) and write (4 files) were used.

claims.claim: Style properties may differ from actual Smelter API (medium confidence)
claims.evidence: User notes correctly flag uncertainty about camelCase style props, absolute positioning model, and color alpha format. These are legitimate concerns when working without documentation.

claims.claim: Duplicate registerOutput call is a bug
claims.evidence: Lines 80-100 register 'program_output' without a root, then lines 103-124 register it again with a root. The user notes acknowledge this as a low-confidence area. This is likely incorrect API usage.

uncertainties: Style property naming (camelCase vs snake_case) is uncertain
Absolute positioning model may differ from CSS
Color alpha format (#RRGGBBAA) may not be supported
Rescaler component name and usage uncertain
RTP config object shapes may differ
needs_review: Duplicate registerOutput call — should likely be a single call with root
Exact style API surface should be verified against SDK types

suggestions: The expectations are quite lenient — all 7 pass despite the executor acknowledging medium confidence and several API uncertainties. Consider adding expectations that check for API correctness (e.g., whether style props use the correct casing, whether position: absolute is valid in Smelter).

The duplicate registerOutput call (lines 80-100 then 103-124) is a functional bug that no expectation catches. An expectation like 'registerOutput is called exactly once per output ID' would catch this.
Consider adding an expectation about whether Rescaler is used correctly, since the executor flagged low confidence about it.
An expectation verifying the pipeline setup (init, registerInput, registerOutput with root) would test the full integration pattern.
eval_feedback.overall: All 7 expectations pass. The code is structurally correct and demonstrates the right general pattern for Smelter scene composition. However, the expectations only test surface-level component usage and miss deeper API correctness issues. The executor's self-reported medium confidence and the duplicate registerOutput bug suggest the expectations could be more rigorous. The user notes are honest and well-organized about uncertainties.
